title: External state needs environment proof
description: Production, staging, and local state may look similar in config; writes require proof of target environment and approval.
body: Use when commands, scripts, migrations, cloud resources, or credentials can target multiple environments. Do not rely on default environment guesses. Actions: inspect explicit endpoint, account, namespace, project, database, and dry-run mode. Validation signals include non-secret environment identifiers and plan output. Risk boundary: unknown target environment blocks execution. Use when Production Change Risk Controls work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
